---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

# About

I'm Buddy — an analytics and data professional by day, hockey obsessive by night.

I've spent my career building data pipelines, models, and frameworks that turn
messy raw data into something actionable. At some point I realized I was doing
the same thing on weekends, except the data was NHL play-by-play and the
stakeholders were entirely imaginary.

That's where this site came from.

---

## Why This Exists

Hockey analytics has made real progress over the past decade. Corsi normalized
shot attempts. Expected goals added context to those attempts. Score-state
adjustments made both metrics more honest. Each generation of work built on the
last by asking the same question: *what is the current metric not capturing?*

That's the question I keep asking. Not because the existing metrics are wrong,
but because every aggregation hides a distribution — and the distribution is
usually where the interesting stuff lives.

The frameworks I build here start from raw play-by-play, do the math from
scratch, and try to be explicit about every assumption along the way. If
something is arbitrary, I'll say so. If a metric has a known limitation, it'll
be in the post. The goal is analysis you can actually argue with.

---

## The Work

Everything on this site is open. The notebooks, the data pipelines, the
formulas — all of it lives on [GitHub](https://github.com/corsi-isnt-enough/nhl-analytics)
and is free to fork, critique, and build on. If you find a flaw in the
methodology, I want to know about it.

Current projects:

- **Special Teams Rating (STR)** — a per-60, score-state-adjusted replacement
  for PP% and PK%
- **Sequence-Based Consistency Metrics** — measuring how teams behave across
  a season, not just in aggregate

---

## Get In Touch

The best way to engage is through GitHub — open an issue, submit a PR, or
just fork the repo and take it somewhere new.

*More frameworks in progress. Check back.*
